howItWorks.intro: This annuity calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: What is an annuity?
faq.answer: An insurance product where you pay a lump sum and receive guaranteed periodic payments. Fixed annuities offer a set rate; variable annuities tie returns to investments.

faq.question: Are annuities a good investment?
faq.answer: Annuities provide guaranteed income but have higher fees and less flexibility than index funds. They are best for people who want guaranteed lifetime income and have maxed out other retirement accounts.

faq.question: How are annuity payments taxed?
faq.answer: The portion representing your original investment is not taxed. The interest/gains portion is taxed as ordinary income. In a qualified annuity (IRA), all withdrawals are taxed.

faq.question: What happens if I die early?
faq.answer: Depends on the type. Life-only annuities stop at death. Period-certain annuities pay beneficiaries for the remaining term. Joint-life annuities continue to a surviving spouse.

faq.question: Is the Annuity Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Annuity Calculator?
sections.body: The Annuity Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your premium paid, annual rate, payout period, you get instant results including monthly payment, total payments, total interest earned. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting monthly payment right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your premium paid and need to find the right monthly payment. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

tips: Double-check your premium paid before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
